events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON only. Need decide pass/fail. Scenario: double-tapped breakers (non-listed). Panelboard installation & makeup, rough-complete. NEC Art. 408 (switchboards, switchgear, panelboards), 110.26 working clearance, 200.4 (terminals must be identified for conductor termination; maybe double-tapping only if listed for multiple conductors). Double-tapped breakers non-listed is non-compliant. Decision fail. Risk high? Double-tapped breakers can cause overheating, arcing, fire. Industrial site. Risk high. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. percent_complete rough-complete maybe 75. value 0. confidence 0.95. event_time 0, rate 0, sound_source "", order [], workable []. findings: double-tapped breakers not listed for multiple conductors; improper termination can cause overheating, arcing, fire. actions: remove and properly terminate each conductor on listed breaker/lug; replace breakers with listed multi-wire or add circuits; verify torque and labeling. rationale: NEC requires terminations per listing; double-tapping non-listed breakers violates 110.3(B) and Art. 408; 200.4 terminal identification. references: NEC 110.3(B), 408.36 etc, 200.4. Need include 110.26? Not directly relevant unless working clearance not mentioned. We can mention working clearance not assessed. But references include NEC Art. 408, 110.26, 200.4. Since scenario includes 110.26, maybe note no clearance issue observed. But decision fail due to double taps.

Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 75,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Circuit breakers are double-tapped with conductors even though the terminals are not listed for multiple conductors.",
    "Improper terminations create high-resistance joints that can overheat, arc, and damage the panelboard.",
    "Condition violates the product listing and installation instructions for the breakers and panelboard."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove the double-tapped conductors from each affected breaker.",
    "Terminate each conductor on a separate listed breaker or on a terminal/lug listed for multiple conductors.",
    "Verify conductor sizing, torque values, and labeling per the manufacturer’s instructions and engineered drawings.",
    "Re-inspect before energizing."
  ],
  "rationale": "Double-tapping breakers that are not listed for more than one conductor violates NEC 110.3(B) and the termination requirements of Art. 408/200.4. This is a fire and arc-fault hazard in an industrial panelboard and must be corrected before close-out.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 110.3(B)",
    "NEC Art. 408",
    "NEC 200.4",
    "NEC 110.26"
  ]
}
